customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety present. Cedric is a motivated, tech-capable new homeowner who self-proposed the appointment. His posture is cooperative throughout. The risk is not resistance — it's split attention on appointment day (deliveries) and an undocumented HOA constraint that could collapse the design.
unresolved_concerns: HOA rear-placement restriction: panels must not be visible from the street. Closer must confirm the design satisfies this before presenting.

No prior bill history: Cedric closed on the house February 24 and has less than one month of usage data. The 182 kWh figure is a single current-day reading, not a monthly average. Closer should set expectations about design accuracy given limited usage history.
Delivery day appointment: Cedric chose March 27 because he has deliveries that day — attention may be split. Closer should open with a brief check-in before diving into the design.
sensitive_topics_raised: New homeowner — less than one month in the house; no established bill pattern yet.
HOA presence confirmed on the property.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for March 27 at noon — phone call with a specialist.
Design will be built before the appointment ('I'm gonna get your design built, and then we will see you on the twenty seventh at noon').
closer_should_know: Cedric's HOA requires panels on the rear of the house, not visible from the street — this constraint was disclosed at the end of the call and is not in the CRM unless Bucky logged it manually. If the closer opens with a design that doesn't account for rear placement, the sit risks collapsing immediately. Confirm rear-placement feasibility before the appointment.
